codeReview.summary: The feature successfully evaluates all 4 mapj_cli skills against documented best practices with a well-structured scoring methodology. The evaluation report fulfills VAL-APP-002 requirements: evaluates each skill, provides numerical scores (70-75/100 range), and identifies gaps with prioritized recommendations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker established a skill evaluation methodology (Discovery Layer 40pts, Execution Layer 50pts, Anti-patterns 10pts) that could be reused for future skill evaluations. This methodology is not documented in AGENTS.md or the skill design guide.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/skill-evaluation-mapj_cli.md sections 1.1-1.4 define the scoring criteria but this is a one-off document, not a reusable reference

sharedStateObservations.observation: The evaluation correctly identifies that mapj_cli skills use `metadata.tags` instead of the emerging standard top-level `keywords` field. This aligns with the synthesis research showing Anthropic/OpenAI/Cursor using top-level keywords.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 4 skill files (skills/mapj/SKILL.md, etc.) use `metadata.tags` - evaluation report section 'Gaps Comunes' item 3 correctly identifies this deviation

sharedStateObservations.observation: The analysis-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker read existing skill files, applied evaluation criteria, produced structured output with tables and recommendations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows worker followed procedure: read mission files → read library files → read skill files → create synthesis document → commit

summary: PASS - The evaluation of mapj_cli skills is comprehensive, accurate, and fulfills all VAL-APP-002 requirements. All 4 skills (mapj, mapj-confluence-export, mapj-protheus-query, mapj-tdn-search) are evaluated with consistent scoring methodology, gaps are correctly identified (missing Role, Success Criteria, keywords field), and prioritized recommendations are provided. No blocking issues found.
